message:
feat(label_intel): normalize cross-label flow to case-insensitive labels

Adds canonical_label() helper and applies it to cross-label flow
matrix computation, ensuring "Backend" and "backend" are treated as
the same label in dependency flow analysis.

Also reformats time-travel cursor bounds check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -257,7 +257,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-8vgr","title":"Implement --label flag subgraph filtering (Go parity gap)","description":"The --label flag currently only computes label health metadata and includes it in the output envelope (label_scope, label_context fields). It does NOT actually filter the issue graph to the label's subgraph before running analysis.

The Go tool (main.go:1458-1471) calls ComputeLabelSubgraph() which replaces the issues list with only those in the label's connected component, then reruns analysis on the filtered set. This means --robot-insights, --robot-plan, and --robot-priority return label-scoped results in Go but full-graph results in Rust.

IMPACT: When a user runs 'bvr --robot-plan --label backend', they expect to see only backend-related issues in the plan. Currently they get the full plan with a metadata tag saying label_scope=backend.

APPROACH:
1. Add a compute_label_subgraph() function that extracts issues with the given label plus their transitive dependencies
2. In main.rs, after computing label_scope, replace the issues vec and rebuild the analyzer with filtered issues
3. Ensure triage, plan, insights, and priority all operate on the filtered set
4. Add conformance tests for label-scoped output

AFFECTED COMMANDS: --robot-insights, --robot-plan, --robot-priority (as documented in Go --label flag help text)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-26T01:22:46.641365520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:03:28.137303801Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:03:28.137109764Z","close_reason":"Implemented compute_label_subgraph() with full connected component walk. Integrated into main.rs to replace analyzer with scoped issues when --label is set. 4 tests, 78/78 conformance. (commit c04fcaa)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","cli","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-9f0","title":"Analysis engine unit tests + conformance + e2e","description":"WHAT: Comprehensive test coverage for all analysis engine additions. THREE layers: (1) Unit tests per module — ImpactScore computation with known graph fixtures (verify each of 8 components), TriageScoringOptions weight application, StaleCleanup detection accuracy, WhatIf delta correctness, advanced insights (TopKSet, CoverageSet, KPaths, CycleBreak) against hand-calculated expected results. (2) Conformance tests: generate Go reference fixtures for triage scoring on shared test graphs, compare Rust output. (3) E2e script (scripts/test_analysis_parity.sh) that builds test .beads/ directories with known issue graphs, runs both bv --robot-triage and bvr --robot-triage, compares scoring order and component breakdown. LOGGING: Every test should log the input graph structure, expected scores, actual scores, and delta. On failure, dump the full graph and all intermediate metric values. FIXTURES: Create at least 3 test graphs — (a) simple linear chain (5 nodes), (b) complex with cycles and clusters (20+ nodes), (c) edge case with single node and empty graph. ACCEPTANCE: All analysis modules have >90% line coverage. Conformance fixtures match. E2e script passes. PARENT: bd-ybj","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:25:55.273518217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T18:26:01.825812552Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T18:26:01.825309139Z","close_reason":"Added 75 new analysis unit tests: suggest.rs (22 new: duplicates, missing deps, labels, cycles, helpers), forecast.rs (20 new: median, complexity, velocity, confidence, agents), alerts.rs (19 new: stale thresholds, in_progress multiplier, cascade severity, filters, summarize), label_intel.rs (18 new: velocity, freshness, flow, criticality, composite health). Fixed 2 snapshot tests. Total lib tests: 619.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9f0","depends_on_id":"bd-ybj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:17.838693001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9f0","depends_on_id":"bd-ybj.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:18.251138512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9f0","depends_on_id":"bd-ybj.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:18.658695140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9f0","depends_on_id":"bd-ybj.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:19.121367924Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9f0","depends_on_id":"bd-ybj.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:19.463560700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":47,"issue_id":"bd-9f0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING STRATEGY: Analysis tests are the most important because scoring bugs are silent — wrong scores don't crash, they just produce bad triage recommendations. Every test should use deterministic graph fixtures (no randomness). Use property-based testing where possible (e.g., all ImpactScore components sum to total, WhatIf deltas are symmetric). The 3-graph fixture set (linear, complex, edge-case) should be shared across all analysis tests. Go conformance may not be exact due to floating-point — use epsilon comparison (1e-6).","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:28:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-aeae","title":"Make BACKGROUND_THRESHOLD configurable for graph analysis phasing","description":"In src/analysis/graph.rs line 256, BACKGROUND_THRESHOLD is hard-coded to 200:

  pub const BACKGROUND_THRESHOLD: usize = 200;

This determines when the analyzer spawns a background thread for expensive metrics (betweenness, HITS, eigenvector, k-core). Below 200 nodes, everything runs synchronously.

WHY 200:
The Go tool uses a similar threshold. At 200 nodes, betweenness centrality (O(V*E)) starts taking noticeable time. Below that, sync computation is fine.

WHEN TO CHANGE:
- Fast machines could handle 500+ nodes synchronously
- Slow machines or CI might want 100
- Users with --force-full-analysis already bypass the phasing entirely

APPROACH:
- Add to .bv/config.yaml as experimental.background_threshold
- Default to 200 for backward compatibility
- Document in --robot-help output","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:11.999859406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:48:52.282375274Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:48:52.281860760Z","close_reason":"background_threshold() reads BVR_BACKGROUND_THRESHOLD env var (commit 70885a0)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","configurability","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aeae","depends_on_id":"bd-24mj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:08.668984122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -270,6 +270,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-hzhd.1","title":"Use relative dates in test fixtures to avoid staleness-dependent failures","description":"Many test fixtures use absolute dates like ts('2025-01-15T10:00:00Z') or '2026-03-08T18:00:00Z'. These dates interact with staleness calculations (14-day warning, 30-day critical, 90-day cap) in triage.rs and alerts.rs.

As time passes, what was once a 'fresh' test issue becomes 'stale', potentially changing test results. Currently the tests pass because the staleness normalization uses relative time, but some assertion values may drift.

AFFECTED FILES (sample):
- src/model.rs lines 568-674 (8+ test fixtures with 2025 dates)
- src/analysis/triage.rs test fixtures
- src/export_sqlite.rs lines 1136-1201 (2026 dates)
- src/analysis/causal.rs test fixtures

APPROACH:
- Replace absolute timestamps with chrono::Utc::now() - Duration offsets
- Use helper functions like 'days_ago(n)' for clarity
- Ensure staleness-sensitive tests use dates relative to 'now'
- Keep conformance fixture dates absolute (they match Go reference output)

RISK: Changing dates in test fixtures may require updating many assertion values. Should be done carefully with snapshot updates.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:25.017690744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:18:54.554686602Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:18:54.553492372Z","close_reason":"Added days_ago() helper in model.rs. Analysis showed existing absolute dates are NOT causing failures because staleness is computed relative to Utc::now(). The helper enables better practice for future tests. (commit 14a5319)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["stability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hzhd.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hzhd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:25.017690744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hzhd.2","title":"Isolate history mode tests from real git repo state","description":"The TUI history mode tests (snap_history_git_mode, e2e_journey_history_deep_dive) read from the actual git history of the bvr repository. This means:

1. The commit count changes with every new commit (now redacted via redact_git_commit_count)
2. Commit SHAs in snapshots reference real commits that may be rebased/squashed
3. The test depends on running from within the bvr git repo

CURRENT MITIGATION:
- redact_git_commit_count() (added commit 9f8f265) masks the volatile commit count
- Snapshots capture the current rendering without asserting specific commit SHAs

IDEAL FIX:
- Create a temporary git repo in the test with known commits
- Set BvrApp.repo_root to the temp repo
- This makes the test fully deterministic and independent of the real repo

COMPLEXITY: Medium — requires understanding how the TUI discovers and reads git history, and setting up a realistic-enough temp repo with commits that reference test issue IDs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:39.957070215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:55:50.683851573Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:55:50.683106944Z","close_reason":"Expanded redact_git_volatile() to mask SHAs, dates, and commit counts in history snapshots (commit 558bba1). Full temp-repo isolation deferred as low-priority since redaction provides sufficient stability.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["stability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hzhd.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hzhd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:39.957070215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hzhd.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hzhd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:02.151189447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
